methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oxford Quantum Circuits, founded by Peter Leek, is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 peers tracked across public and private markets: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Q-CTRL.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to peer-set constraints. The Stage-Progression method uses the $100M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $190M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable here: while 7 same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap emerged in same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier classifications—only 1 same-stage comparable was identified—making a narrow peer median unreliable. A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a median point of $190M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable Series B-stage quantum computing transactions and the nascent, pre-revenue profile typical of the sector. Investors should weight this estimate accordingly and supplement with operational milestones (qubit count, error rates, customer traction) to refine conviction.
